title: SACRAMENTO, CA –

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Telehealth modalities, such as phone and video calls, have been critical in providing health care during COVID-19.",
    "AB 32 seeks to make flexible rules for the use and reimbursement of telehealth services permanent under state law.",
    "The passage of AB 32 aims to increase access to health care services for all Californians, including vulnerable families and Medi-Cal beneficiaries.",
    "Telehealth has highlighted how instrumental online medical care is to the health and wellbeing of Californians during the pandemic.",
    "Telehealth will continue to exist long after COVID-19 and should be accessible to every Californian.",
    "Telehealth has transformed the health care delivery system by eliminating barriers to in-person care, including the need for transportation and child care.",
    "Telehealth has expanded access to time-sensitive sexual and reproductive health care during the pandemic.",
    "Remote visits have resulted in shorter wait times for appointments and drops in no-show rates, particularly in areas with provider shortages.",
    "Telehealth has become a critical component of health care during the COVID-19 pandemic and is proving to be important in perpetuity.",
    "Telehealth has reduced long-standing barriers to care, especially for vulnerable populations such as Medi-Cal patients and individuals living in rural regions.",
    "COVID-19 has shown that telehealth is integral to an equitable health delivery system for communities of color."
  ]
}
```
